title: The Next Field

features.name: eucalyptus-burl
features.desc-on-touch: a tree with a memory casing nested in its knot, still broadcasting, still waiting. the children walked past it twice and didn't know it was calling them both times.

features.name: fence-fragment
features.desc-on-touch: a line of posts, some still upright, some fallen, the boundary between one family's use of the earth and another's. the children crossed it twice.

features.name: trail-marker
features.desc-on-touch: where the children's footsteps wear the grass away into a pale line. the mark reads as a name, or a question, or both at once.

features.name: forgotten-shed
features.desc-on-touch: a structure at the field's far edge, windows reflecting sky, door hanging. the children thought about entering but didn't. the archive is still thinking about what they saw.

features.name: rain-pool
features.desc-on-touch: a low place where water collects and the children sometimes stood, watching themselves multiply in the mirror. the pool remembers both versions of them.

features.name: stone-arch
features.desc-on-touch: a gate or doorway made of weathered stone, part of a structure long ruined. the children passed through without understanding they were crossing a threshold.

inscription: the next field is not empty; it holds the shape of the children who walked into it and never came back out the same

file.name: Property_Deed_Fragment_And_Sketches_On_Blank_Reverse.jpg
file.note: a land survey corrupted with child drawings: route maps, stick figures, the space between two places rendered as a creature

exits.label: back through the fence-line and neighbour's property toward the original absence
onward to the next neighbour's fence, where the trail continues bleeding into others' land
exits.to: NEW: the parallel field, where another set of children are always just leaving

exits.label: toward the distant tree line, where the trees are old enough to know what the children forgot
back toward town, following the route the children took when they finally decided to leave
